meta.title: MS-PBI-4: Semantic Models, Visual Design, and Governance

questions.stem: A Power BI author wants transformations to be pushed back to the source system whenever possible so refreshes stay efficient. Which Power Query concept is MOST relevant?
questions.explanation: **Query folding** is correct because it pushes supported transformation logic to the source system, which can improve refresh efficiency. **Row-level security**, **cross-highlighting**, and **bookmarks** solve different report and security needs.

questions.stem: A semantic model must be easy to understand and perform well for analytics. Which modeling approach is generally recommended for Power BI?

options.text: Design a star schema with fact tables and dimension tables
Duplicate each dimension once per report page
Use only many-to-many relationships between all tables
Store every attribute in one wide flat table regardless of grain

questions.explanation: **Design a star schema with fact tables and dimension tables** is correct because Microsoft recommends star schema design for usability and performance in Power BI semantic models. The other options create ambiguity, poor maintainability, or unnecessary complexity.

questions.stem: A report needs to slice a sales fact table by a date table. For a standard dimension-to-fact design, which relationship direction is usually preferred?

options.text: A one-to-many relationship from the dimension table to the fact table
A many-to-one relationship from fact to dimension as the primary design pattern
No relationship; rely on duplicated columns in visuals
A bidirectional many-to-many relationship by default

questions.explanation: **A one-to-many relationship from the dimension table to the fact table** is correct because a dimension table usually contains unique keys that filter the many rows in the fact table. Defaulting to many-to-many or duplicated fields makes models less clear and often less performant.

questions.stem: A data analyst must calculate total margin that changes correctly with report filters and slicers. Which semantic model object should they create?
questions.explanation: **A measure** is correct because measures are evaluated at query time in the current filter context, making them appropriate for dynamic calculations. Themes, alerts, and workspace roles do not define reusable analytical calculations.

questions.stem: A DAX author needs to modify filter context when evaluating an expression. Which function is the core tool for that task?
questions.explanation: **CALCULATE** is correct because it evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, which is foundational in DAX. **FORMAT**, **RELATEDTABLE**, and **UNION** have other uses and are not the primary filter-context modification function.

questions.stem: A report designer wants selections in one visual to affect another visual differently on the same page. Which feature should they configure?

options.text: Tenant settings for publish to web

questions.explanation: **Edit interactions** is correct because it controls how one visual filters, highlights, or does not affect another visual on the same report page. Refresh history, sensitivity labels, and tenant settings are unrelated to per-visual interaction behavior.

questions.stem: An analyst wants to compare the contribution of categories to a whole at a single point in time. Which visual is usually the most appropriate?

options.text: A pie or donut chart when the number of categories is small
A decomposition tree as the default for simple part-to-whole comparison
A scatter chart for univariate composition

questions.explanation: **A pie or donut chart when the number of categories is small** is correct because these visuals are intended for simple part-to-whole comparisons with limited categories. Scatter charts, gauges, and decomposition trees are meant for different analytical tasks.

questions.stem: A company needs different sales managers to see only their own region in the same semantic model and reports. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: **Row-level security (RLS)** is correct because it restricts data visible to different users based on role filters. Parameters, bookmarks, and alerts do not enforce user-specific data visibility in the service.

questions.stem: A dataset contains several years of fact data, but only the newest partitions change regularly. Which feature can reduce refresh time by processing only recent data?
questions.explanation: **Incremental refresh** is correct because it partitions data and refreshes only the configured recent range instead of reloading the entire dataset each time. The other features do not optimize dataset refresh processing.

questions.stem: A report must be collaboratively developed by a team before it is packaged for broad consumer access. Where should authors usually work first?

options.text: In Excel comments attached to the dataset
Only in each author's personal My Workspace for shared development

questions.explanation: **In a workspace** is correct because workspaces are the collaborative development and management container for Power BI content before publishing apps to consumers. **My Workspace** is personal, and the other options are not collaborative content management spaces.
